digest.top_line: TREASURE: consolidative thoracic RT added to atezo maintenance gave no OS benefit (mOS 6.7 vs 13.4mo, HR 1.55, ns) and halted early for excess fatal toxicity in ES-SCLC.
digest.tldr: Thoracic carried the day's only signal, and it's a cautionary one: TREASURE shows consolidative TRT (30 Gy/10 fx) layered on atezo maintenance in unselected ES-SCLC gives no OS benefit (HR 1.55, ns) and tripled fatal AEs (19.4% vs 3.0%), halted early. The CREST-era TRT benefit doesn't survive the immunotherapy backbone.

sites.intro: The day's single trial tests whether the pre-IO consolidative-TRT benefit holds on an atezolizumab maintenance backbone.

studies.name: TREASURE
studies.tldr: mOS 6.7 vs 13.4mo (HR 1.55, ns) with consolidative TRT added to atezo maintenance; halted early for excess toxicity in unselected ES-SCLC.
details: 📊 1° endpoint OS not met: adding consolidative TRT gave no survival benefit and numerically worse OS

details.text: ⚠️ Toxicity by arm (TRT+atezo vs atezo alone)

details: 🔍 Consolidative thoracic RT 30 Gy/10 fx (arm A); both arms on atezolizumab maintenance after carbo-etoposide-atezo induction
🔍 Phase 2 open-label RCT, 20 sites Germany/Austria; ES-SCLC with ≥SD post-induction
⚠️ Halted early at 68 of planned 104 pts (34/arm) on SMC advice; OS underpowered (needed 66 events, 92 pts)

subdetails: Persistent lymphocyte depletion after TRT, proposed driver
Lower DLCO in arm-A pts with fatal AEs; no other risk factor found, baseline balanced

details: 🔗 vs CREST (pre-immunotherapy): consolidative TRT gave modest OS benefit; not reproduced when layered on atezo maintenance

verdict.rationale: Randomised phase 2 halted early for excess TRT toxicity; harm signal significant (SAEs P<.001) though OS underpowered. Argues against routine consolidative TRT.

studies.significance: The harm concentrated in pts with lower baseline DLCO and post-TRT lymphopenia, pointing to pulmonary reserve as the selection axis if consolidative TRT is revisited. For now, omit consolidative TRT after chemo-IO in unselected ES-SCLC: 30Gy/10fx to an unselected thorax added infections and deaths, not survival.
studies.monday_clinic: In unselected ES-SCLC still responding after carbo-etoposide-atezo induction, this questions routine consolidative thoracic RT on atezolizumab maintenance; it leaves open whether a fitter, better-pulmonary-reserve subset could tolerate it.

primary_endpoint.name: Overall survival
primary_endpoint.stat_detail: HR 1.55 (95% CI 0.90-2.69), P=.34; primary OS not met

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Phase 2 open-label randomized (1:1) trial at 20 sites in Germany and Austria; recruited Sept 2020 to Aug 2022. Powered for 66 events / 92 pts assuming a 20% absolute 12-mo OS gain (72% vs 52%, HR 0.57).

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: Unselected ES-SCLC with at least stable disease after induction carboplatin-etoposide-atezolizumab. Baseline characteristics balanced between arms and between pts with and without SAEs.

analysis_sections.label: Radiotherapy
analysis_sections.body: Consolidative thoracic RT 30 Gy in 10 fractions (arm A) added to atezolizumab maintenance; arm B received atezolizumab maintenance alone.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: mOS 6.7 vs 13.4 mo, HR 1.55 (0.90-2.69), P=.34, numerically worse with TRT. mPFS 2.4 vs 2.6 mo, HR 0.92 (0.54-1.55), P=.85.

analysis_sections.label: Safety
analysis_sections.body: Severe AEs 61.3% vs 18.2% (P<.001); fatal outcomes 19.4% vs 3.0% (P=.04). Infection and respiratory SAEs predominant; persistent post-TRT lymphopenia and lower DLCO in fatal-AE pts implicated.

analysis_sections.label: vs leading data
analysis_sections.body: CREST showed a modest OS benefit for consolidative TRT in the pre-immunotherapy era; TREASURE fails to reproduce it when TRT is layered onto atezolizumab maintenance.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Unselected ES-SCLC responding to chemo-IO induction. Does not address a pulmonary-reserve-selected subgroup, which the trial was too small and underpowered to define.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Open-label; halted early at 68 of 104 planned pts on safety-committee advice, leaving OS underpowered. Post hoc survival update performed April 2026.

open_questions: Which patients can receive consolidative thoracic RT without excess infection risk

related_trials.brief_title: Radiotherapy to All Residual Lesions After Chemoimmunotherapy
related_trials.brief_summary: Extensive-stage small-cell lung cancer is a lethal malignancy with an extremely poor prognosis. First-line chemotherapy could only achieve an overall survival of approximately 10 months. CREST study demonstrated that the addition of thoracic radiotherapy to the patients who responded to chemotherapy could increase the 2-year survival rate from 3% to 13%. related_trials.brief_title: Lymphocyte Depletion and Change in Lymphocyte Functionality
related_trials.brief_summary: Lymphocytes are a type of white blood cell (WBC) responsible for adaptive immunity. Thoracic tumors are adjacent to many blood/immune rich organs including the great vessels, heart, thoracic-spine, and lymph-node-stations. During radiation treatment the impact to lymphocytes can be significant. This may cause a decrease in the amount of lymphocytes. A researcher at UVA has created a system to predict and reduce the immune cell reduction following lung SBRT treatments beyond standard of care.…

papers.title: Consolidative Thoracic Radiotherapy With Atezolizumab Maintenance in Extensive-Stage Small Cell Lung Cancer
papers.abstract: Importance Chemoimmunotherapy followed by immunotherapy maintenance is the standard first-line treatment for extensive-stage small cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC), yet data regarding efficacy and safety of consolidative thoracic radiotherapy (TRT) are lacking. Objective To determine whether combining consolidative TRT with immunotherapy maintenance in ES-SCLC is safe and improves patients’ overall survival (OS) and progression-free survival (PFS). Design, Settings, and Participants This was a multicenter open-label phase 2 randomized clinical trial recruiting patients from September 2020 to August 2022 in Germany and Austria, with the last follow-up visit in September 2024. Eligible patients had ES-SCLC with at least stable disease after induction chemoimmunotherapy (carboplatin−etoposide−atezolizumab). Although the database was locked in April 2025, a post hoc survival update was performed in April 2026. Data were analyzed from April 2025 to April 2026. Interventions Randomized (1:1) to either atezolizumab maintenance combined with consolidative TRT (30 Gy in 10 fractions) (arm A) or atezolizumab maintenance alone (arm B). Main Outcome and Measure OS (time from randomization to death due to any cause). Results Of 96 patients assessed for eligibility, 68 patients were randomized; recruitment was prematurely terminated due to safety concerns. Median OS in the combination arm was numerically shorter compared to atezolizumab only (6.7 months [95% CI, 5.1-9.0] vs 13.4 months [95% CI, 10.7-17.5]; HR, 1.55 [95% CI, 0.90-2.69]; P = .34), while median PFS was similar (2.4 months [95% CI, 1.3-3.9] vs 2.6 months [95% CI, 1.2-3.9]; HR, 0.92 [95% CI, 0.54-1.55]; P = .85). TRT plus atezolizumab was accompanied by higher frequency of severe adverse events (SAEs) (19 patients [61.3%] vs 6 patients [18.2%]; P &amp;lt; .001) and fatal outcomes (6 patients [19.4%] vs 1 patient [3.0%]; P = .04). Safety analysis revealed predominance of infection and respiratory disorder−related SAEs, possibly facilitated by a depletion of lymphocytes observed specifically in patients after TRT, and by a lower single breath diffuse capacity of the lungs for carbon monoxide in patients with fatal AEs in arm A. No further risk factors were identified, given that baseline characteristics were well balanced between both arms and between patients with and without SAEs, including fatal SAEs. Conclusions and Relevance In this randomized clinical trial, TRT combined with immunotherapy increased toxic effects in unselected patients with ES-SCLC without survival benefit, presumably due to radiation-induced lymphocyte depletion facilitating infections. Cautious patient selection and further investigation to identify those who may benefit without undue risk are required. Trial Registration ClinicalTrials.gov Identifier: NCT04462276
